slides.title: The tiny shin muscle that *prevents falls*.
slides.sub: Your tibialis anterior lifts your toes with every step. Train it and you trip far less often.

slides.title: Meet your tibialis anterior
slides.body: It runs down the front of your shin and *lifts your foot* upward. Every step depends on it, yet almost no one trains it.

slides.title: Toe clearance keeps you upright
slides.body: A weak tibialis lets your toes drop, so they catch the ground. Better clearance *can reduce* the trips that cause falls.

slides.title: Falls are a longevity threat
slides.body: After 65, a single fall can change everything. Stronger toe lift *is linked to* steadier walking and more independence.

slides.title: Train the lift, not the push
slides.body: Most calf work trains pushing off. Tibialis raises and band pulls train *the lifting motion* your stride forgets.

slides.title: Two easy moves at home
slides.body: Sit, loop a band over your foot, and pull your toes up. Or stand and *lift your toes* against a wall. Daily.

slides.title: Build a stride that *stays steady*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your movement, steps, sleep and food, turning small daily habits into one clear Health Score you can act on.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The trips that lead to falls often start with one overlooked muscle in your shin. 🦵

Train your tibialis anterior so your toes clear the ground, and you walk steadier for decades. Save this. 🔖
